title: carthamus tomentosus
intro: Carthamus tomentosus, also known as woolly safflower or woolly thistle, is a flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its soft, woolly leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The health benefits of Carthamus tomentosus include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant properties that can help to protect against cell damage. Carthamus tomentosus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, fever, and digestive problems.
